title: Federal Spending by Agency — FY2026
h1: Federal contract spending by agency — FY2026 to date
metaDescription: Which federal agencies spend the most on contracts in FY2026 to date. Ranked by obligations from USAspending.gov, led by Department of Defense.

narrative.intro: Federal contract obligations through FY2026 to date total $474B. The Department of Defense leads obligations with $239B, more than any other agency. Other large obligors include the Department of Homeland Security at $52B, the Department of Veterans Affairs at $51.1B, and the Department of Energy at $45.7B. Civilian procurement and services spending is visible in the General Services Administration at $18.6B and the Department of Health and Human Services at $13.5B. Several mission agencies and departments record single-digit billions in obligations: NASA at $9.5B, the Department of Transportation at $8.7B, and both the Department of Agriculture and the Department of Justice at $6.3B each. These figures represent obligations recorded in FY2026 to date; total award value figures reflect full contract values rather than single-year obligations. The ranking highlights where procurement dollars are concentrated across defense, homeland security, veterans services, energy, and civilian agency buying, providing a snapshot of federal contracting priorities through the current fiscal reporting period.

sections.heading: Which agencies have the largest contract obligations?
sections.body: The Department of Defense has the largest obligations at $239B. Other high-obligation agencies include the Department of Homeland Security at $52B, the Department of Veterans Affairs at $51.1B, and the Department of Energy at $45.7B. The General Services Administration and Department of Health and Human Services follow with $18.6B and $13.5B respectively.

sections.heading: What do the top obligation amounts say about federal procurement focus?
sections.body: Defense and security-related agencies dominate the top of the list, led by the Department of Defense at $239B and the Department of Homeland Security at $52B. Health, energy, and civilian acquisition are also represented by the Department of Veterans Affairs at $51.1B, the Department of Energy at $45.7B, and the General Services Administration at $18.6B. Mission-specific agencies such as NASA at $9.5B and the Department of Transportation at $8.7B appear lower on the ranking.

faq.q: Which federal agency spends the most on contracts?
faq.a: The Department of Defense spends the most on contracts, with $239B in obligations. The next largest obligors are the Department of Homeland Security at $52B and the Department of Veterans Affairs at $51.1B. Together with the Department of Energy at $45.7B and other agencies listed, these figures compose the top-ranked federal contract obligations for FY2026 to date.
